fields.description:
## Context / Problem

The eval/calibration surface excludes project code-review criteria. `_code_review_prompt_id`
resolves against a static built-in set with no `repo_root`/gate awareness
(`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:187-201`), and `calibrate_criterion` has only a plan-review
branch — it maps ids via `criterion_prompt_id` but never for the code-review gate
(`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:416-420`). So an activated project code-review criterion has no eval
path (fourth of five seams; root cause on superseded bug 8a4b).

## Approach

Make eval resolution gate-aware, reusing the resolver from the foundation child:

- In `eval_solver.py`, extend `_code_review_prompt_id`, which receives a **physical** prompt id
  (e.g. `code-review-project-foo`) and today admits it only if it is in the static built-in
  physical set. Note the id-space mismatch: `registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` (exists at
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py:179-184`) returns **logical** ids (e.g. `project.foo`), so
  it cannot be membership-tested against a physical id directly. Build the admissible **physical**
  set by mapping each logical id from `effective_criteria(repo_root)` through
  `criterion_prompt_id(cid, gate_key="code_review")` — the gate-keyed resolver added by child 1
  `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` (`project.foo` → `code-review-project-foo`) — and admit the incoming
  physical id iff it is in `(static built-in physical set) ∪ (mapped project physical set)`. Thread
  `repo_root` (already available and passed to `_run_code_review_case` at `eval_solver.py:204-234`).
  This ticket therefore **depends on** child 1 for the gate-keyed `criterion_prompt_id`.
- In `eval.py` `calibrate_criterion`, add a code-review branch that resolves the physical prompt id
  via `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review")` — the gate-keyed parameter added
  by child 1 `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` — when the id belongs to the code-review gate, then
  loads its eval spec through the existing `load_eval_spec(prompt_id, repo_root=repo_root)` path.
- A project criterion with no eval spec raises a **located** error naming the criterion.

**Rejected alternative.** A separate code-review eval entrypoint — duplicates `calibrate_criterion`
and its solver wiring; instead reuse the single solver, keyed on the resolved gate.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` — `_code_review_prompt_id` repo_root/gate-aware admission.
- `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py` — code-review branch in `calibrate_criterion`.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — extend this shared test file (created earlier
  in the stack). This ticket depends on child 1 `[rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]` for the gate-keyed
  `criterion_prompt_id`; its tests build a synthetic repo with a `project.foo` code-review criterion
  plus a `.eval.md` spec.

## Testing

- Happy path (eval): the synthetic repo's `project.foo` resolves to `code-review-project-foo` and
  calibrates through the code-review arm (`_run_code_review_case`).
- Id-mapping (the T1/G6 seam): assert `_code_review_prompt_id("code-review-project-foo")` is
  admitted (non-None) when `project.foo` is active, and that a physical id for an *inactive* logical
  id is rejected — pinning that admission maps logical→physical, not a raw-string compare.
- Parity: a built-in plan-review id calibrates exactly as today (plan-review branch unchanged).
- Edge (missing spec): a project code-review id with **no eval spec** raises a located error that
  names the criterion.

Tier: unit with the synthetic-repo fixture; the model call inside calibration is stubbed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An activated project code-review criterion resolves and calibrates through the code-review
      arm; verified by
      `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k eval`.
- [ ] A plan-review criterion's eval path is unchanged (parity); verified by the same `-k eval`
      selector asserting the plan-review branch still resolves `plan-review-<id>`.
- [ ] `_code_review_prompt_id` admits the physical id `code-review-project-foo` for active logical
      `project.foo` (logical→physical mapping, not raw compare) and rejects an inactive one;
      verified by the same `-k eval` selector.
- [ ] A project code-review id with no eval spec raises a **located** error naming the criterion
      (does not silently pass or emit a generic error); verified by the same `-k eval` selector
      asserting the raised error type and that the message contains the criterion id.
